char.name: Rachel
char.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

friends.name: Jacob
friends.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

friends.name: Laban
friends.bio: Of Haran. Family is family, and a good deal is a good deal. My sister marries well.

friends.name: Joseph
friends.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

friends.name: Benjamin
friends.bio: Youngest of the twelve. My mother's last breath named me son of sorrow, but my father calls me son of his right hand.

friends.name: Leah
friends.bio: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧

friends.name: Tamar
friends.bio: Widow of Judah's house, wronged and made to wait. I will secure my rightful place in this family, one way or another.

friends.name: Abram
friends.bio: Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.

friends.name: Lot
friends.bio: Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.

friends.name: Isaac
friends.bio: Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑

friends.name: Rebekah
friends.bio: Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨

friends.name: Esau
friends.bio: Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹

friends.name: Judah
friends.bio: Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁

posts.author: CHAR_171
posts.memorial_for: Rachel, beloved of Jacob, who died on the road to Bethlehem
posts.body: I have lost her. We were on the road near Bethlehem when Rachel went into labor, and it went wrong, and it would not stop going wrong. She gave me her last son as her own life ran out of her, and with the final breath in her body she named him Ben-oni, son of my sorrow. I could not let the boy carry that grief as a name his whole life, so I call him Benjamin, son of my right hand. But I know what she named him. I was holding her hand when she said it. I buried the love of my life beside the road to Bethlehem and set a pillar of stones over her grave, and I stood there with a newborn in my arms and could not tell whether my heart was breaking or bursting. Seven years I worked for her. It was not nearly enough time with her. It was never going to be enough.
posts.highlight: Rachel dies bearing Benjamin and is buried on the road to Bethlehem (Gen 35:16-20)
posts.ember: Rachel's grave near Bethlehem; her weeping for her children echoes in Jeremiah 31:15 and at Bethlehem in Matthew 2:18

comments.author: CHAR_187
comments.body: She held me and Benjamin both in the same hour, the older and the newborn, and then she was gone. I will carry her with me the rest of my life, father. I promise you I will look after my little brother. Always.
comments.time: the first watch of the night

author_info.name: Joseph
author_info.bio: Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨

author_info.name: Jacob
author_info.bio: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

posts.author: CHAR_173
posts.body: We are leaving, and we are leaving in secret, because you do not give a man like my father a chance to say no. The men have the flocks moving already; the children are loaded onto the camels. And I have done something I have not told my husband. On my way out of my father's house, I took his household gods, the little idols he keeps and swears by, and I hid them in my baggage. I cannot fully explain to you why. Part of me wanted to wound him the way he has wounded us for twenty years. And part of me, God help me, still half believes the little things carry luck and protection. I was raised in that house too. Some of Haran is coming with me whether I want it to or not.
posts.highlight: Rachel steals Laban's household gods as they flee (Gen 31:17-19)
posts.ember: the pull of the old gods; idolatry smuggled out of Haran will have to be buried before Bethel (Gen 35:2-4)

author_info.name: Rachel
author_info.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

posts.author: CHAR_173
posts.body: God remembered me. After everything, the years, the jealousy, the shame of an empty tent while my sister filled the camp with sons, God remembered me and opened my womb, and I have a boy of my own. I named him Joseph, and I will tell you exactly what I meant by the name: God has taken away my disgrace, and, may the LORD add to me yet another son. Even now, even in the happiest hour of my whole life, I am already asking for one more, because that is what long waiting does to a woman, it never quite lets you rest. But not today. Today I am not going to spoil it with wanting. Today I just hold him. My son. Mine.
posts.highlight: God remembers Rachel; Joseph is born (Gen 30:22-24)
posts.ember: Joseph, who will one day save this whole quarreling family alive; Rachel's 'add another son' foreshadows Benjamin, and her death bearing him

author_info.name: Rachel
author_info.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍

posts.author: CHAR_173
posts.body: Everyone in this camp keeps count of my sister's sons and I cannot give my husband even one, and it is eating me alive from the inside. I said something ugly to Jacob. I told him, give me children or I will die. And he turned on me, hard: am I in the place of God, who has kept you from having children? So I gave him my maid Bilhah to bear on my knees, and then Leah gave hers, and now there are baby boys everywhere in this camp and not one of them is mine. It has gotten small and bitter between my sister and me. Her boy found mandrakes in a field, and I actually traded her a night with our own husband to get them. That is what Leah and I have become. Two sisters bartering one man and a handful of roots, both of us starving for the same two things: a child, and to be the one he loves. There is only enough of him for one of us. We both know which one it is.
posts.highlight: Rachel's jealousy; the handmaids Bilhah and Zilpah; the mandrakes traded for a night with Jacob (Gen 30:1-16)
posts.ember: the bitter rivalry of the sister-wives; the twelve tribes of Israel born out of a house at war with itself

author_info.name: Rachel
author_info.bio: Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍
